title: Dam security-door panels render white — monitor-screen billboard SHADE is lit (G_LIGHTING on) instead of unlit; NOT the CI-palette class
suspect: src/platform/fast3d/gfx_pc.c:~16989 (G_LIGHTING branch treats vertex colour as a normal)
src/game/chrobjhandler.c:28020 process_monitor_animation_microcode (tvscreenRender) builds the screen quad
src/game/model.c:10035 modelRenderNodeDl (visible DLCOLLISION render, uses baked Primary DL)
repro: build-webgpu/ge007 --level dam --deterministic --config-override Video.TexturePack= with GE007_AUTO_WARP_FRAME=20 GE007_AUTO_WARP_PAD=6 GE007_AUTO_WARP_PAD_FORWARD_OFFSET=-120 GE007_AUTO_WARP_PAD_Y_OFFSET=40 --screenshot-frame 95 --screenshot-exit. --deterministic is MANDATORY (pcMaybeApplyScriptedWarp bails without it) and the HD texture pack must be disabled (it masks the bug).

evidence.path: src/platform/fast3d/gfx_pc.c:21732 grayscale-I8 fallback renders Dam security-door control panels (PROP_SEC_PANEL bondconstants.h:2826, PROP_DOORPANEL :3071, PROP_DOORCONSOLE :3072) white/gray instead of retail green/red. Root cause is the known CI-palette fallback class, not a missing door-state->color coupling. The port decodes CI (palette-indexed) textures at draw time via a per-texturenum cached palette: gfx_pc.c:21673 (if fmt==G_IM_FMT_CI: pal=texGetPalette(texturenum,&ncolours)); :21691 decodes with color only if (pal && ncolours>0); :21732-21740 else-branch '/* No palette -- fallback to grayscale I8 */' writes R=G=B=raw_index_byte,A=255 -> near-white for an indicator texture. Palettes are keyed to texturenum ONLY on the zlib/compressed-paletted load path (image.c:685 texStorePalette; store image.c:341, lookup image.c:370). CI textures whose TLUT arrives via runtime G_LOADTLUT (gfx_dp_load_tlut gfx_pc.c:20677) or a shared/static TLUT are never texStorePalette'd -> texGetPalette returns NULL -> grayscale. Same class already fixed+tooled for Dam guard faces + alarm lens (tools/dam_palette_regression.sh: fails on 'No palette | fallback to grayscale', asserts alarm lens >=150 red px); the door-panel texturenums are an uncovered set in the same class. Render path: prop-model DL rewriter unk_0CC4C0.c (texture resolve :1632-1638, TLUT via gDPLoadTLUT06/07) -> gfx_pc.c:21673 CI decode. Door STATE lives at DoorRecord.openstate bondtypes.h:2954 / keyflags :2913, but no prop-color coupling exists (grep-confirmed); retail color is baked in the texture. OPEN QUESTION (needs ares/N64 RDP capture toggling door locked->open): whether retail color is static (baked, likely) or also state-driven via a monitor TVCMD_SETCOLOUR path (chrobjhandler.c:28133). Either way the WHITE symptom is the CI-palette fallback.

history.note: User-reported (2026-07-12) Dam security-door buttons white instead of green/red. Root-caused via read-only investigation agent. Fix pattern established (guard faces/alarm lens): register the runtime-G_LOADTLUT palette keyed to the pending texturenum, then add door-panel texturenums to dam_palette_regression COLOR_TEX_IDS. Needs a Dam capture to pinpoint the offending texturenum(s) and a visual/oracle check for the correct color. | UPDATE 2026-07-12 (experiment): H1 UNREPRODUCED headless -- ~15 captures (Dam --level 33 start at AUTO_WARP_CHRNUM 0/2/3/6/9/12/15/18/39/40 + AUTO_WARP_PAD 4/20/40/80, all identical start scene; the warp never relocated to interior door consoles; plus a full boot+menu capture, 200 frames/102k lines) showed 0 CI grayscale fallbacks (SETTEX_CI pal=nil), 0 unregistered-static-texture TLUT loads, 0 fix firings. Every runtime TLUT observed (TEX-TLUT-STATIC, e.g. tex=1980) already had a registered palette. Implemented then REVERTED a candidate fix (register the base!=NULL runtime-TLUT palette under rdp.texture_to_load.static_texture_num in gfx_dp_load_tlut; gated GE007_NO_RUNTIME_TLUT_PALETTE_FIX). NOT landed -- speculative, fires nowhere reachable, and correctness rests on static_texture_num==image's num at LOADTLUT (confirmed only for the already-registered tex=1980, not the door case). H2 (flat-quad apptype==1 white prop, explosions.c:130) or a lighting/reachability factor are now competing hypotheses. TO CONFIRM: a LIVE or interior-route capture of a Dam security-door console with GE007_TRACE_TEX_PIPELINE + GE007_VERBOSE -- check SETTEX_CI pal=nil on the console texture; if H1 confirmed, re-apply the reverted fix + a log at gfx_pc.c:20748 and validate before/after screenshots + dam_palette_regression + renderer parity.

history.evidence: ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED — the prior CI-palette-grayscale-fallback hypothesis (H1) is REFUTED. Reproduced the exact white panel (pad 6, room 111; screenshot confirms gray frame + blank white screen). Across the capture: ZERO [CI-GRAYSCALE-FALLBACK] and ZERO SETTEX_CI pal=nil — the door panels never touch the CI/palette path. They are PROPDEF_MULTI_MONITOR screen billboards. GE007_MONITOR_TRACE proves the SIM is correct: the screen colour cycles red (220,40,40) <-> green (50,200,50) <-> off (50,50,50) on a 32x32 fmt=0 (RGBA, non-CI) texture, drawn with combine G_CC_MODULATEIA (= TEXEL0 x SHADE). The screen renders WHITE because SHADE is lit away: a new GE007_TRACE_MONITOR_SHADE probe shows the per-vertex screen colour reaching gfx_pc.c's shade loop (~gfx_pc.c:16989) with G_LIGHTING=1, so the RGB is reinterpreted as a NORMAL and d.color becomes the lit colour: green (71,192,83)->(150,150,150), red (185,64,83)->(255,255,255). Retail rendered this quad UNLIT (SHADE = vertex colour = the activation colour). No palette anywhere. FIX DIFFICULTY (why in-DL clears fail): the visible screen renders from the model's BAKED Primary DL via modelRenderNodeDl -> gSPDisplayList (portLookupDlColRwData FAILS for these nodes, so process_monitor's runtime body — which carries the sim colour and could clear lighting — is used ONLY for collision, not the visible draw). Clearing G_LIGHTING in the emitter (process_monitor) and at the visible render (modelRenderNodeDl, before the gSPDisplayList) both had NO effect (Primary re-establishes the lit state and/or a different path renders it). A gfx_pc.c fix keyed on the monitor model's vertex-data span + saturated screen colour did NOT reliably match: the screen verts are loaded from per-frame-varying source addresses across a multi_monitor model, and the colour also cycles through light 'off'/white states. RECOMMENDED FIX (focused follow-up): make the multi_monitor screen quad render UNLIT in the visible path — either route process_monitor's runtime body (sim colour + a G_LIGHTING clear) to the VISIBLE DLCOLLISION render instead of only collision (fix the runtime-vs-baked selection in modelRenderNodeDl/portLookupDlColRwData for monitor nodes), or tag the screen draw so gfx_pc.c skips lighting for exactly those verts. TOOLING added this session (kept): [CI-GRAYSCALE-FALLBACK] detection log at gfx_pc.c (the fallback previously fired silently; the dam_palette_regression 'No palette' grep was DEAD) + the regression now asserts on it. The MON-SHADE / warp repro is documented above.
history.note: 2026-07-13: deep root-cause pass. The ledger's original CI-palette hypothesis is WRONG. Real cause = monitor-screen billboard rendered lit (G_LIGHTING) so its per-vertex activation colour is treated as a normal -> white. Reproduced + mechanism confirmed; fix is a focused render-path change (multi_monitor DLCOLLISION runtime-vs-baked / unlit screen quad).
